title: UTCDate: add parse() returning DateTime<Utc>
description:
What: Add a parse() method to UTCDate that returns a parsed DateTime<Utc>, and add a convenience as_datetime() method.

Algorithm:
1. In crates/jmap-chat/src/jmap.rs, add to the UTCDate impl block:
   /// Parse the stored string as RFC 3339 and return a DateTime<Utc>.
   pub fn parse(&self) -> Result<chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>, crate::error::ClientError> {
       chrono::DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(&self.0)
           .map(|dt| dt.to_utc())
           .map_err(|e| crate::error::ClientError::Parse(e.to_string()))
   }
2. Add chrono imports at the top of jmap.rs: use chrono::{DateTime, Utc}; (or use fully qualified paths in the method if preferred).
3. Confirm ClientError::Parse exists (it does: Parse(String) variant).
4. Add a unit test in the jmap.rs #[cfg(test)] block:
   Test name: utc_date_parse_valid
   Input: UTCDate::from_trusted("2024-01-02T12:00:00Z")
   Expected: parse() returns Ok with year=2024, month=1, day=2
   Oracle: hand-computed expected values from the literal string, not from the code under test.
   Test name: utc_date_parse_invalid
   Input: UTCDate::from_trusted("not-a-date")
   Expected: parse() returns Err(ClientError::Parse(_))
5. Run: cargo test -p jmap-chat

Defensive rules:
- Do not change UTCDate's internal representation (keep it as String newtype).
- Do not change serde behavior of UTCDate.
- Do not add parse() to the constructor — it is a separate method on existing instances.
- to_utc() converts FixedOffset to Utc correctly.

Tests:
- parse("2024-01-02T12:00:00Z") → DateTime<Utc> with expected year/month/day values.
- parse("not-a-date") → Err(ClientError::Parse(...))
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p jmap-chat passes including new utc_date_parse_valid and utc_date_parse_invalid tests. cargo clippy -p jmap-chat -- -D warnings passes.
